codeReview.summary: Fix adequately addresses VAL-AUDIT-012. Both soft delete (marking inactive when service requests exist) and hard delete (when no service requests exist) now create audit_event entries. The implementation uses createAuditEvent with action='delete', proper entityType='procedure_type', actorType='admin', and includes ipAddress/userAgent from extractClientInfo. A test file with 5 tests covers both delete paths and queryability.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The handoff mentions BACKEND_STATUS.md was updated to document audit behavior for procedures. Checking the diff confirms the update at line 157 adds: '- **audit events for all mutations**: create, update, and delete (both soft and hard delete) create `audit_event` entries with `action` and `payload`'. This follows the governance rule to update BACKEND_STATUS.md when backend behavior changes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: BACKEND_STATUS.md diff shows documentation added in procedures section (around line 157)

sharedStateObservations.observation: SkillFeedback indicates followedProcedure=true with no deviations. The worker used the backend-domain-worker skill and followed the documented procedure correctly.

summary: Fix-audit-procedure-delete successfully resolves VAL-AUDIT-012. The remove handler in procedures.router.ts now instruments both soft delete (when hasRequests=true) and hard delete (when hasRequests=false) paths with createAuditEvent calls. Each audit event includes actorType='admin', entityType='procedure_type', action='delete', descriptive summary via buildProcedureSummary, and payload with mode/reason indicators. The commit also adds client info tracking (ipAddress, userAgent) to all audit events across the codebase, plus a new audit router with list/get endpoints, and a comprehensive audit page UI. Five tests were added to verify the procedure delete audit behavior.
